uncertainty-a.title: Critical uncertainty A
uncertainty-a.prompt: Choose an uncertainty that would significantly change strategic choices, and name both end states.
examples: Customer budgets grow ↔ customer budgets freeze

uncertainty-b.title: Critical uncertainty B
uncertainty-b.prompt: Choose a second independent uncertainty. Avoid describing the same force as A.
examples: Technology matures fast ↔ technology matures slowly

scenario-1.title: Scenario 1
scenario-1.prompt: If one end of A and one end of B both happen, what market emerges? Which BMC blocks are pressured?
examples: Name the scenario, key signals, opportunities, threats, and business-model implications

scenario-2.title: Scenario 2
scenario-2.prompt: Write the second scenario. Focus on how customers, channels, partners, or revenue logic change.
examples: Which value propositions strengthen? Which cost structures break?

scenario-3.title: Scenario 3
scenario-3.prompt: Write the third scenario. Do not make it merely the pessimistic version.
examples: Who benefits in this future? Who loses? Which options do we hold?

scenario-4.title: Scenario 4
scenario-4.prompt: Write the fourth scenario and identify the strategic logic that differs most from the others.
examples: Which assumptions should be tested earliest? Which signals should be monitored?

robust-moves.title: Robust moves / early signals
robust-moves.prompt: Which moves are useful across multiple scenarios? Which external signals reveal that a scenario is forming?
examples: Monitor regulatory pilots, budget-freeze rate, and technology cost curve
